title: Design an explicit pagination contract
description: Define how clients page through collections from the start, choosing cursor or offset semantics and a stable response envelope.
body: Use when an endpoint returns a collection that can grow. Do not return an unbounded list with no paging contract. Actions: choose cursor-based paging for large or changing data and offset only for small stable sets; define the page size, the cursor or page parameters, and where the next link lives; keep ordering stable. Validation signals include a client iterating all pages without missing or duplicating items as data changes. Risk boundary: an unbounded list endpoint becomes a performance and reliability hazard at scale, so commit to a paging contract before launch.
